mechanism_narrative: A low and steady FEDFUNDS reading below recent inflation implies an easing stance, which suppresses real-rate expectations and pulls the 10-year Treasury yield lower over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day Treasury yield changes are not negative in at least 60% of periods with FEDFUNDS below CPIAUCSL inflation pressure proxies, or if the rolling 2-year sign accuracy falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales and payroll growth both accelerate, the market infers stronger aggregate demand and higher future nominal growth, which lifts the 10-year Treasury yield within 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises above its prior 3-month average and PAYEMS rises above its prior 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce positive 20-day Treasury yield changes more than 55% of the time, or if either RSAFS or PAYEMS alone explains the sign equally well without the interaction.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening labor demand and softer growth expectations, which reduces term premium pressure and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield down over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise episodes are followed by negative 20-day Treasury yield changes in fewer than 55% of cases, or if the sign flips positive during repeated validation windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in INDPRO combined with a rebound in HOUST indicates stronger real activity and capacity utilization, which raises near-term rate expectations and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and HOUST both rise versus their prior 2-month averages
failure_condition: Reject if concurrent INDPRO and HOUST improvements fail to correspond to positive 20-day Treasury yield changes in at least 60% of triggered cases, or if the interaction term adds no predictive value over single-series effects.

mechanism_narrative: An inflation acceleration in CPIAUCSL while FEDFUNDS remains unchanged creates a policy-lag narrative of tighter real policy ahead, which lifts the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds FEDFUNDS by at least 1 percentage point
failure_condition: Reject if CPI inflation spikes with unchanged policy do not lead to higher 5-day Treasury yield changes in at least 55% of occurrences, or if yields instead fall systematically after the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in GDPC1 relative to the prior quarter signals weakening final demand and lower expected policy rates, which lowers the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDP contractions are not followed by negative 60-day Treasury yield changes more often than positive ones, or if the effect disappears after accounting for recent CPI and labor data.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized softening in PAYEMS and a rising UNRATE signals labor-market deterioration that weakens growth expectations, so the 10-year Treasury yield falls within 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls for 2 consecutive months while UNRATE rises versus its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the combined deterioration signal fails to produce negative 20-day Treasury yield changes in at least 60% of triggered observations, or if the result is driven by only one proxy.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, a rising FEDFUNDS rate after inflation has already firmed in CPIAUCSL produces a stronger real-rate and duration-pressure channel, which pushes the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if, during tightening cycles, 60-day Treasury yield changes are not positive in at least 55% of cases when FEDFUNDS rises alongside firm CPIAUCSL, or if the sign is persistently negative in validation.
